title: Healthcare Administrator

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling optimization, billing oversight and routine compliance reporting are largely automatable, but service-line strategy, regulatory judgment and staff leadership are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Healthcare-specific revenue-cycle, staffing-optimization and compliance-analytics products are credible and improving fast, short of the embedded maturity of coding copilots.
rationales.adoption: AI-driven scheduling and revenue-cycle tools are now common in large health systems and spreading into mid-size practices.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agentic workflows can already run scheduling, claims triage and standard compliance reporting end to end, with escalations still routed to a human administrator.

tasksAtRisk: Agentic claims triage and billing oversight
tasksGrowing: Healthcare strategy and service line development
Patient experience and quality improvement leadership
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-9111 (Medical and Health Services Managers), p25-p75 rounded
insight: Agentic tools now run scheduling, billing triage and standard compliance reports end to end with light oversight, cutting the back-office headcount these functions once required. Administrators who lead AI implementation and own regulatory and quality strategy are taking on more scope, not less.
restatement: v2's realization ceiling (task automation as a hard cap, agentic factor weighted at only 10%) scores this role lower than v1's flatter composite; back-office administration is more agent-automatable than ever, but core management judgment still caps the ceiling well below where v1 placed it.
